sections.title: What are Question Tags?
points: A question tag is added to the end of a statement to check or confirm information.
Positive sentences usually end with a negative tag.
Negative sentences usually end with a positive tag.

sections.title: How to Choose the Tag
points: Use the same auxiliary verb from the sentence.
If there is no auxiliary, use do/does/did.

sections.text: Remember: 'I am' uses the special tag 'aren't I?'.

cards.fact: Positive statement + negative tag, negative statement + positive tag.
cards.mnemonic: Mirror the sentence with the opposite tag.

cards.fact: The helper verb stays the same in the tag.
cards.mnemonic: Can → can't, will → won't, do → don't.

cards.fact: I am uses 'aren't I?' instead of 'amn't I?'.
